The '''IPV-1 System Patrol Craft''', also known as the '''Imperial patrol vessel''',<ref name="P&P" /> '''Imperial patrol vehicle'''<ref name="SOTGSaga">[[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'', Saga Edition]]</ref> or '''''IPV-1''-class corvette''', was a standard [[Star system/Legends|system]] security and customs vessel commonly used near [[Planet/Legends|planets]] to interdict [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and protect against [[Pirate/Legends|piracy]]. It was produced by [[Republic Sienar Systems/Legends|Republic Sienar Systems]].

==Description==
Being very fast and maneuverable, they patrolled [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] and later [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] when larger ships were unavailable, and were capable of keeping pace with the small, fast, and heavily armed pirate ships that preyed on hyperlanes. They were thus regarded as the first line of defense against purveyors of contraband and various organizations attempting insurrection against a system, and relied on their speed to catch and disable or destroy enemy craft. Pirate attacks fell to an all-time low when this craft was in a sector. However many fell into the hands of pirates and crime syndicates who used them to defend their outposts.

[[File:IPV-1_SFS_SPC_XwA.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]] IPV-1 System Patrol Craft.]]

Lacking a stock [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], the IPV-1 was often deployed along specific patrol routes along known smuggling lanes to maintain the peace and order within controlled space. At least some IPV-1s, like the [[Freejack]], were modified for hyperspace travel.<ref name="SOTGSaga" />

In order to serve their purpose well, these vessels were armed with four [[Taim & Bak/Legends|Taim & Bak]] [[XV7 turbolaser]]s, as well as a single [[Krupx Munitions/Legends|Krupx]] [[VL-4a warhead launcher]].

==History==
At some point the ''IPV-1'' was the most common warship in the Imperial Navy and skirmish [[Line of battle|lines]] of up to 24 IPVs guarded systems that required a permanent imperial presence.<ref name="TEGTW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>

The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] was also known to have purchased them through various merchant systems.

One was present at [[Jerrilek]] during the [[Mission to Graleca|rescue]] of [[Chel Dorat]].

During the [[Thrawn campaign]], [[Niles Ferrier]] managed to steal the three IPV-1 System Patrol Craft from the [[Cavrilhu Pirates]] in the [[Amorris system/Legends|Amorris system]] in order to sell them to the Empire.<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref><ref name="DFR">''[[Dark Force Rising]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'']] claimed that these ships were originally manufactured for the Republic and that their original designation was "Independent Patrol Vehicle". The name was changed to "Imperial Patrol Vehicle" under the Empire, but the design of the ships was not.<ref name="SOTGSaga" />

In ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]'', IPV-1s may be built at pirate docks, but not Imperial bases; they are also seen very often in [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] pirate fleets orbiting pirate controlled planets in Empire at War's "Galactic Conquest" mode.

The IPV-1 was mispelled IVP-1 in ''[[Loyalties]]'' and [[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'', Saga Edition]].
